provenance: Hand-computed. Bailey & López de Prado 2014, 'The Deflated Sharpe Ratio: Correcting for Selection Bias, Backtest Overfitting, and Non-Normality', Financial Analysts Journal, Eq. 9. Formula: DSR = (SR_obs - SR_0) * sqrt(T-1) / sqrt(1 - gamma3*SR_obs + (gamma4-1)/4 * SR_obs^2). SR_0 = 0 (null hypothesis). Independently verified in Python.
description: Positively-skewed returns: gamma3=1.0 (right-skew), gamma4=4.0 (mild excess kurtosis). SR_obs=1.5, T=252, SR_0=0. Positive skew (gamma3>0) reduces the denominator, inflating DSR relative to the normal case — skewed strategies look better than they are under naive Sharpe.
